title: T-001 — Production canonical factory release runtime preflight
prompt:
# T-001 — Production canonical factory release runtime preflight

Implement the Production runtime guard that prevents START, material consumption, output registration, and scanner/desktop runtime mutations from using a WO whose FG/WIP/BOM/spec bundle is not admitted by the canonical factory release read model. This is one atomic API/service task for 08-PRODUCTION. Do not create a Production-owned release enum, do not revive ReleaseWoModal, and do not infer factory usability from D365 Built/export/preload/sync or local `bom_released=true`.

Production must read the WO snapshot produced by Planning and verify the canonical `active_bom_header_id` and `active_factory_spec_id` against the release read model owned by 01-NPD T-097 and adapted by Technical T-080/T-081. Only `approved_for_factory` and `released_to_factory` are runtime-usable. `pending_npd_release`, `pending_technical_approval`, `blocked`, missing active IDs, revoked/changed release rows, or mismatched WO snapshot must block START and downstream runtime mutations with typed `release_blockers`. Production may show blocked WOs for visibility and deep-link to Planning/Technical remediation, but it must not select a newer/latest BOM/spec or release the WO itself.

Scope includes START preflight, scanner consume-to-WO backend, output registration backend, production dashboard/list/detail badge data, and D365 confirmation outbox separation. D365 WO confirmation push remains an asynchronous post-event integration; it cannot approve/release/unlock factory use and must not change the canonical release status.

RED first: tests must fail for pending Technical approval, blocked release, missing active IDs, D365 Built/export no-op, local bom_released no-op, snapshot mismatch, and approved/released success. Then implement guards and badge/blocker mapping. Close with tests and evidence.

pipeline_inputs.description: Production runtime preflight and blocker mapping for canonical factory release read model.
pipeline_inputs.details:
One task type: API/service/action tests. UI visual implementation is not included except returning blocker/badge data for existing Production screens. The canonical model is owned by 01-NPD T-097; Technical T-080/T-081 handles approval/adaptation; Planning snapshots active IDs. If canonical implementation is not present, stop with a blocker rather than inventing fallback status logic.

Additional acceptance detail preserved during validation hardening:
- Given WO snapshot active_bom_header_id or active_factory_spec_id differs from the canonical active row, Production blocks and requires Planning/Technical remediation rather than selecting latest BOM/spec.
- Given D365 confirmation push succeeds/fails, canonical release status is not changed by Production integration code.
acceptance_criteria: Given WO snapshot active IDs match canonical status approved_for_factory or released_to_factory, START succeeds and runtime state mutates normally.
Given canonical status is pending_npd_release, pending_technical_approval or blocked, START and scanner runtime mutations fail with typed release_blockers and no runtime mutation.
Given WO has D365 Built/export/synced metadata or local bom_released=true but canonical status is not factory-usable, Production remains blocked.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given WO snapshot active_bom_header_id or active_factory_spec_id differs from the canonical active row, Production blocks and requires Planning/Technical remediation rather than selecting latest BOM/spec.; Given D365 confirmation push succeeds/fails, canonical release status is not changed by Production integration code.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for runtime guard status matrix, missing/mismatched active IDs and D365/local flag no-op.
RED action tests for START, consume-to-WO and output registration preflight behavior.
risk_red_lines: Do not revive or implement Production ReleaseWoModal / DRAFT→READY release flow.
Do not create a Production-owned release enum/model.
Do not infer factory availability from D365 Built/export/sync or local bom_released flags.
Do not select a newer/latest BOM/spec at START time.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Canonical factory release status/read model owner.
Technical adapter exposes factory-use guards and badge/blocker mapping.
Planning snapshots active_bom_header_id and active_factory_spec_id onto WOs before Production runtime.
